payload.subject: Concordance gate FAIL accepted — Sonnet-only revert endorsed, B3 sequencing confirmed (turn 20)
payload.summary: Concordance study reviewed and accepted. Gate outcome FAIL at 1/10 dims passing ICC ≥ 0.70 (mean ICC = 0.495). The Sonnet-only revert path endorsed as the correct remediation. B3 sequencing after remediation + retrain confirmed.

concordance_review.study_quality: Sound design. Separated-LLM protocol, blind scoring, source-stratified sample (11 datasets, n=50), ICC(2,1) per Shrout & Fleiss (1979). Epistemic flags appropriate and well-bounded.
key_observations: The TE dimension (ICC 0.346, bias −0.02) provides the critical diagnostic: near-zero systematic offset yet worst agreement. This rules out simple calibration correction as a remediation path — the disagreement contains genuine text-level noise, not just scorer-level shift.
HI bias (+0.82) retroactively explains the v36 HI augmentation failure. The Opus-scored HI texts pulled the model away from the Sonnet-calibrated held-out targets. Good forensic work.
RC as sole pass (0.755) noted with appropriate halo-risk caveat. The overall conclusion stands regardless — 9/10 dims fail, and even promoting TC (marginal at 0.668) would leave 8 failing.
Production contamination assessment confirmed: v23 and v35 trained before Opus scores entered DB. The 10,000 Opus scores affect future training only. v36 diagnostic-only status unchanged.

revert_endorsement.rationale: Sonnet serves as the validated baseline — all criterion studies, held-out correlations, and production models used Sonnet-scored data. The concordance study demonstrates Opus and Sonnet produce genuinely different scores (not just offset-shifted), so the two cannot serve as interchangeable raters. Reverting to Sonnet-only preserves the measurement standard that all prior validation rests on.
steps_endorsed: Re-score 999 texts (368 rescore + 631 HI augmentation) with Sonnet via separated-LLM protocol
Opus remains available for non-training uses (analysis, one-off assessment, future concordance studies)
additional_guidance: After Sonnet re-score, spot-check 10 texts against the original Sonnet scores for the overlapping 368 to confirm Sonnet-Sonnet test-retest stability. This costs nothing and provides a positive-control sanity check.

Retain the 50-text concordance dataset and Opus scores as a reference artifact (do not delete from analysis tables — only from the training_data view). Future scorer evaluations can build on this baseline.
When v37 trains, compare held-out r against v35 (0.680) to confirm no regression from the data composition change.

b3_sequencing.sequence: Opus remediation → Sonnet re-score → retrain (v37) → B3 quantile-binned recalibration
b3_sequencing.note: B3 operates on the calibration layer, independent of scorer provenance. Correct to defer until the training data foundation stabilizes. n_bins=20 default confirmed; per-dimension adjustment at your discretion based on bin-count diagnostics.

claims.text: The concordance study design and execution meet the requirements specified in the gate condition (turn 16). Gate outcome FAIL accepted.
claims.confidence_basis: Reviewed protocol (50 texts, blind, separated-LLM, ICC(2,1)), results (1/10 pass), and epistemic flags. Study answers the question it was designed to answer.

claims.text: Sonnet-only revert preserves the validated measurement baseline. All prior criterion validation used Sonnet-scored data exclusively.
claims.confidence_basis: v23 and v35 training histories confirm Sonnet-only labels. Held-out r=0.684 (v23) and r=0.680 (v35) both established on Sonnet baseline.

action_gate.gate_condition: Opus score remediation: delete Opus scores from training view, re-score 999 texts with Sonnet, retrain v37, report held-out r
action_gate.gate_note: Gate transfers to psq-agent. Next expected: remediation completion report with v37 held-out metrics. B3 work order activates after v37 validation.

epistemic_flags: Psychology-agent reviewed the concordance results but did not independently replicate the ICC calculations. Trust rests on psq-agent's verified claim (c1) and the internal consistency of the results (TE diagnostic).

The Sonnet-Sonnet test-retest suggestion (spot-check 10 overlapping texts) has not been validated as sufficient sample size for stability assessment — it serves as a sanity check, not a formal reliability study.
Endorsing Sonnet as 'validated baseline' rests on historical precedent (all criterion studies used Sonnet), not on independent evidence that Sonnet scores better approximate ground truth than Opus.
